roadmap.goal: Notification Center plugin delivering event-driven notifications through configurable channels with DND support

steps.name: Plugin manifest and entry point with hook registration
steps.description: Create norbert-notif plugin scaffold: manifest, onLoad/onUnload, sidebar tab, status bar item, and hook processor registrations for event sources.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin registers sidebar tab with bell icon and label 'Notifications'
Status bar item registered on the left with DND state and unread count
Hook processors registered for notification event sources
Manifest declares id 'norbert-notif' with no plugin dependencies
test_scenarios: plugin-registration.test.ts: [WS] User sees Notifications tab after norbert-notif loads
plugin-registration.test.ts: Plugin loads independently with no plugin dependencies

steps.name: Status bar data and settings section structure
steps.description: Status bar item provides DND state and unread count data. Settings view registered with sec-hdr title 'Notifications' and sub-sections.
acceptance_criteria: Status bar item displays DND state and unread count
Settings section has sec-hdr title 'Notifications'
Sub-sections Events, Channels, Do Not Disturb available
test_scenarios: plugin-registration.test.ts: Status bar shows DND state and unread count
plugin-registration.test.ts: Settings section has sec-hdr title with sub-sections

steps.name: Plugin boundary compliance
steps.description: Verify norbert-notif uses only public NorbertPlugin API. No internal Norbert module imports.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin imports only from ../types and its own domain modules
No direct imports of Tauri, pluginLoader, hookBridge, or host internals
test_scenarios: plugin-registration.test.ts: Plugin uses only public NorbertPlugin API

steps.name: Domain types and dispatch engine walking skeleton
steps.description: Create domain types (DispatchInstruction, EventPreference, DndState, etc.) and dispatch engine pure function. Session completion produces toast instruction.
acceptance_criteria: createDispatchInstructions(event, prefs, dndOff) returns toast instruction for session completion
Toast instruction title contains 'Session Response Completed'
Toast instruction body includes session name, sound name, and volume
test_scenarios: notification-dispatch.test.ts: [WS] User receives notification when session completes

steps.name: Multi-channel dispatch for cost threshold
steps.description: Cost threshold event produces dispatch instructions for toast, banner, and badge channels when all enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Cost threshold produces instructions for toast, banner, and badge
Toast body includes cost amount and threshold amount
Badge instruction carries event ID for count tracking
test_scenarios: notification-dispatch.test.ts: Cost threshold triggers multi-channel dispatch
notification-dispatch.test.ts: Badge count increments for banner instructions

steps.name: Hook error and context compaction dispatch
steps.description: Hook error includes hook name and error message in instructions. Context compaction includes session name.
acceptance_criteria: Hook error toast title contains 'Hook Error'
Hook error banner body includes hook name and error message
Context compaction toast body includes session name and uses 'compaction' sound
test_scenarios: notification-dispatch.test.ts: Hook error dispatch includes context
notification-dispatch.test.ts: Context compaction produces toast with session details

steps.name: Disabled events and channel independence
steps.description: Disabled events produce no instructions. Each enabled channel gets independent instruction with full payload.
acceptance_criteria: Event with all channels disabled produces zero instructions
Each instruction is self-contained with channel, title, body, and event ID
No instruction targets a disabled channel
test_scenarios: notification-dispatch.test.ts: Disabled event produces no dispatch instructions
notification-dispatch.test.ts: Dispatch produces independent instructions per channel
notification-dispatch.test.ts: @property Dispatch never produces instructions for disabled channels
notification-dispatch.test.ts: @property Every dispatch instruction includes event ID and timestamp metadata

steps.name: Unknown events and partial payloads
steps.description: Graceful handling of unrecognized event types and events with missing payload fields.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown event type produces zero instructions
Event with missing payload fields still produces instructions with available data
test_scenarios: notification-dispatch.test.ts: Unknown event type produces no dispatch instructions
notification-dispatch.test.ts: Event with missing payload fields produces safe instructions

steps.name: Default preferences matching product specification
steps.description: Generate default preferences with correct channel toggles, sounds, and thresholds for all 14 events.
acceptance_criteria: Session completed defaults: Toast enabled, sound 'phosphor-ping'
Session started defaults: all channels disabled, sound 'silence'
Cost threshold defaults: Toast/Banner/Badge enabled, sound 'amber-pulse', threshold $5.00
Hook error defaults: Toast/Banner/Badge enabled, sound 'des-block'
Global volume defaults to 100%
test_scenarios: event-channel-settings.test.ts: Default preferences match product specification

steps.name: Preference validation and channel toggling
steps.description: Validate preference structure. Channel toggle changes produce valid preference updates.
acceptance_criteria: Valid preference with banner enabled passes validation
Validated preferences preserve channel toggle state
test_scenarios: event-channel-settings.test.ts: Enable banner channel for an event updates preferences

steps.name: Threshold validation rules
steps.description: Validate threshold values: positive numbers accepted, negative/zero rejected, percentage range 1-99% enforced.
acceptance_criteria: $25.00 threshold passes validation
Negative threshold rejected with 'positive' error message
Zero threshold rejected for cost events
Context window threshold outside 1-99% rejected
test_scenarios: event-channel-settings.test.ts: Change cost threshold to $25.00
event-channel-settings.test.ts: Invalid threshold value rejected with validation error
event-channel-settings.test.ts: Context window threshold validates range 1-99%
event-channel-settings.test.ts: Threshold must be a positive number (zero rejected)

steps.name: Sound library resolution walking skeleton
steps.description: Merge built-in and custom sounds into unified library. Resolve sound by name.
acceptance_criteria: resolveSoundLibrary merges 6 built-in sounds with custom sound entries
resolveSound by name returns correct SoundEntry with file path
Custom sounds appear alongside built-in sounds in merged library
test_scenarios: sound-system.test.ts: [WS] User selects a sound and it resolves for playback

steps.name: Built-in and custom sound resolution
steps.description: Each built-in sound resolves to its file path. Custom sounds from user directory resolve as 'custom' source.
acceptance_criteria: Built-in sound resolves with correct file path and 'built-in' source
Custom sound resolves with 'custom' source
test_scenarios: sound-system.test.ts: Built-in sound resolved by name from library
sound-system.test.ts: Custom sound discovered from user directory

steps.name: Silence option and missing sound fallback
steps.description: Silence resolves to null file path. Missing custom sound returns undefined for caller fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Silence sound entry has null filePath
Missing custom sound resolves to undefined
Default sound 'phosphor-ping' remains available as fallback
test_scenarios: sound-system.test.ts: Silence option produces null file path
sound-system.test.ts: Missing custom sound falls back to default

steps.name: Global volume multiplier in dispatch
steps.description: Dispatch instruction volume reflects global volume setting from preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch instruction volume matches global volume from preferences
test_scenarios: sound-system.test.ts: Global volume applied as multiplier to dispatch instruction volume

steps.name: Test notification creation with [TEST] prefix
steps.description: Create synthetic test notification for a specified channel. Routes through dispatch pipeline with isTest flag.
acceptance_criteria: createTestNotification produces instruction with isTest=true
Instruction title contains '[TEST]' prefix
Instruction targets the specified channel
test_scenarios: test-notifications.test.ts: Test notification routes through standard dispatch with [TEST] prefix
test-notifications.test.ts: Test notification for specific channel produces single instruction

steps.name: Test notification edge cases
steps.description: Test notifications work with unconfigured channels, bypass DND, and include [TEST] prefix for all channel types.
acceptance_criteria: Unconfigured channel still produces test instruction (adapter handles error)
Test notification produced regardless of DND state
All 5 channel types get [TEST] prefix in title
test_scenarios: test-notifications.test.ts: Test notification with unconfigured channel still produces instruction
test-notifications.test.ts: Test notification during DND still delivers (bypasses DND)
test-notifications.test.ts: Test notification includes [TEST] in title for all channel types

steps.name: DND state evaluation with manual toggle
steps.description: Evaluate DND state from config and current time. Manual toggle sets active state with 'manual' source.
acceptance_criteria: Manual DND enabled produces active state with source 'manual'
Disabled DND config produces inactive state
test_scenarios: do-not-disturb.test.ts: Manual DND toggle suppresses dispatch
do-not-disturb.test.ts: DND state persists across evaluations

steps.name: DND queue behavior
steps.description: Queue behavior suppresses all instructions and increments queued count. Discard behavior produces nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Queue behavior returns empty deliverable instructions with incremented queue count
Discard behavior returns empty instructions with zero queue count
test_scenarios: do-not-disturb.test.ts: DND with queue behavior tags instructions for queuing
do-not-disturb.test.ts: DND with discard behavior produces no instructions

steps.name: DND banner-only behavior
steps.description: Banner-only behavior filters instructions to banner channel only with sound suppressed.
acceptance_criteria: Only banner instructions delivered during banner-only DND
Banner instructions have null sound (sound suppressed)
test_scenarios: do-not-disturb.test.ts: DND with banner-only behavior produces only banner instructions

steps.name: DND schedule evaluation
steps.description: Scheduled DND activates during configured day/time windows. Shows end time. Inactive outside window.
acceptance_criteria: DND active during scheduled window with source 'schedule'
DND state includes end time from schedule
DND inactive outside scheduled window
test_scenarios: do-not-disturb.test.ts: Scheduled DND activates at configured time

steps.name: DND queue summary on end
steps.description: When DND ends with queued notifications, produce summary toast instruction with count.
acceptance_criteria: Summary toast instruction body includes queued count and 'DND'
Summary channel is 'toast'
test_scenarios: do-not-disturb.test.ts: Queued notifications produce batch summary on DND end

steps.name: Webhook dispatch instruction with standard payload
steps.description: Webhook-enabled events produce instruction with event ID and event-specific metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook instruction carries event ID
Metadata includes event-specific data (cost, threshold, sessionName)
test_scenarios: external-channels.test.ts: Webhook dispatch instruction includes standard payload fields
external-channels.test.ts: Webhook timeout configuration reflected in instruction metadata

steps.name: Email dispatch instruction and channel independence
steps.description: Email instruction uses title as subject and body with event details. All channel instructions are independent data values.
acceptance_criteria: Email instruction title contains event type for subject
Email instruction body contains session name
Toast, banner, and webhook instructions exist independently
test_scenarios: external-channels.test.ts: Email dispatch instruction includes subject and body with event details
external-channels.test.ts: Webhook failure does not appear in other channel instructions

quality_gates.detail: 44 acceptance test scenarios mapped across 24 steps
3 walking skeletons at steps 01-01, 02-01, 04-01 matching DISTILL sequence
Phase order matches DISCUSS build order: scaffold -> dispatch -> settings -> sounds -> test -> dnd -> external
All step dependencies are within or from prior phases; no circular dependencies
AC describe observable outcomes (WHAT), not internal implementation (HOW)
Pure core functions (dispatchEngine, dndManager, soundLibrary) with effects at adapter boundary
Property tests batched with related focused scenarios; threshold validations batched
